categories: Health coverage exemptions: Forms & how to apply
title: Exemptions from the requirement to have health insurance
main-title: Health coverage exemptions: Forms & how to apply
meta-title: Exemptions from the fee for not having coverage
meta-description: Most people must have qualifying health coverage or pay a fee for the months they don’t have insurance. But if you qualify for a health coverage exemption you don’t have to pay the fee
explainertopic: Health coverage exemptions: Forms & how to apply
page_name: Exemptions from the requirement to have health insurance
page_category: Health coverage exemptions: Forms & how to apply
excerpt: Most people must have qualifying health coverage or pay a fee for the months they don’t have insurance. But if you qualify for a health coverage exemption you don’t have to pay the fee
content:
The fee for not having health insurance (sometimes called the "Shared Responsibility Payment" or "mandate”) ended in 2018. This means you no longer pay a tax penalty for not having health coverage. If you don’t have health coverage, you don’t need an exemption to avoid paying a tax penalty.

However, you may be able to get a Catastrophic plan if available in your area if one of these applies to you:

  • Under 30 years
  • Over 30 years and don't qualify for savings on a Marketplace plan
  • You qualify for a hardship or affordability exemption

Learn about exemptions and Catastrophic coverage.

Live in California, Connecticut, the District of Columbia, or Maryland?

These states have their own exemptions processes. Visit their websites to apply for an exemption:

  • If you live in California, visit Covered California.
  • If you live in Connecticut, visit Access Health CT.
  • If you live in the District of Columbia, visit DC Health Link.
  • If you live in Maryland, visit Maryland Health Connection.

Learn more about health coverage exemptions

  • Get exemption forms, and learn how to apply.
  • Review what happens after you apply for an exemption.
